trade.summary: Moderate bilateral trade. Italia exports machinery, chemicals, fashion goods, and food; Malesia exports electronics, palm oil, rubber, and petrochemicals. Malesia is an important manufacturing hub for Italian companies seeking Southeast Asian production. EU-ASEAN trade dynamics frame the broader relationship.

diplomatic.summary: Italia and Malesia maintain normal diplomatic relations. Malesia's non-aligned posture and EU-ASEAN dialogues provide the main diplomatic frameworks. Relations are functional but not especially close. Malesia's Islamic identity and political stances on Middle East issues (vis-a-vis Italia's EU obligations) can create divergences.

military.summary: Limited military ties. Italia has sold naval vessels to Malesia historically. No formal alliance. Defense cooperation through EU-ASEAN frameworks at a general level.

financial.summary: Growing bilateral investment. Italian manufacturing companies use Malesia as a regional production hub. Malesian sovereign wealth fund (Khazanah) has European holdings. Some Italian firms in energy and infrastructure in Malesia.

energy.summary: ENI has some operations in Malesia's oil and gas sector. Malesia is an LNG exporter and Italia is a significant LNG importer; some trade flows exist. Palm oil imports from Malesia are used in Italian food production.

scientific.summary: Moderate scientific cooperation. Some joint research in tropical medicine, palm oil sustainability, and engineering. Italian universities have limited formal exchange with Malesian counterparts.
